sections.title: Executive Synthesis
sections.content:
- Outcome: Expansion to two adjacent segments can add $42-58M ARR over 24 months with controlled CAC payback.
- Growth in core segment is slowing from 22% to 11%, requiring mix diversification.
- Win-rate analysis indicates Segment B has 1.8x conversion versus Segment C at current positioning.
- Main constraint is onboarding capacity, not demand generation; implementation throughput is now the critical bottleneck.
- Recommendation: prioritize Segment B first, then phase Segment C after onboarding redesign in Q3.
- Risk: dilution of enterprise focus; mitigation: dedicated enterprise pod and pricing guardrails.
- Decision required this quarter: approve phased expansion and enablement investment envelope.

sources.label: Internal CRM and pipeline analytics, Jan 2026

sections.title: SWOT Assessment
sections.content:
- Strength: Net retention in enterprise accounts remains 118% with low logo churn.
- Weakness: Time-to-value averages 61 days, 2.2x peer benchmarks.
- Opportunity: Mid-market compliance segment growing 17% annually with low incumbent depth.
- Threat: Competitors bundling implementation services at discounted rates.
- Strategic implication: speed-to-value becomes the primary defensibility lever over feature breadth.

sections.title: Option Tradeoff
sections.content:
- Option A (Broad expansion): fastest top-line upside, highest execution risk, weaker focus.
- Option B (Phased expansion): moderate upside, stronger conversion confidence, manageable capacity risk.
- Option C (Core-only optimization): lowest risk, but limited growth headroom.
- Criteria weighting: growth potential (35), execution risk (30), payback speed (20), strategic fit (15).
- Recommendation: Option B because weighted score is highest under base and downside assumptions.

sections.title: Implementation Roadmap And Risks
sections.content:
- Month 0-2: finalize segment playbook and enable dedicated GTM pod.
- Month 2-4: redesign onboarding workflow and reduce handoff latency by 30%.
- Month 4-6: launch Segment B with weekly conversion and cycle-time governance.
- Month 6-9: readiness gate for Segment C based on capacity and payback metrics.
- Key risk: onboarding queue overload; mitigation: capacity trigger thresholds and contingency staffing.
- KPI set: ARR pipeline quality, conversion, CAC payback, onboarding cycle time.

sources.label: Internal CRM and pipeline analytics, Jan 2026
